summary:
Cholesterol mainly circulates via LDL transporting about two-thirds of total cholesterol to cells where it supports various essential processes; this makes LDL delivery critical compared to other lipid transport mechanisms like those involving HDL. To summarize further while adhering strictly to your instructions but maintaining clarity: 

Cholesterol chiefly moves through blood using LDL particles, supplying nearly all tissue needs with crucial components including hormones and cell membrane integrity factors more so than being returned directly by HDL. This highlights why LDL’s primary function stands out as paramount among cholesterol transportation tasks in human physiology. Agent1 concludes based heavily upon established medical knowledge regarding how different types of lipids manage cholesterol distribution throughout our bodies efficiently yet distinctly according their specific properties and destinations inside us.
